description:
Advances: specled.realized_by, specled.mix_tasks

Deliverable:
The ADR was authored at distill time (.spec/decisions/specled.decision.realized_by_tier_implication.md exists). The spec edits to realized_by.spec.md and mix_tasks.spec.md were also authored at distill time. This stage performs the author-side dedup demonstrating spec scenario 2:
- Edit .spec/specs/binding.spec.md to remove SpecLedEx.Realization.Binding.resolve/2, SpecLedEx.Realization.Canonical.hash/1, and SpecLedEx.Realization.HashStore.read/2 from realized_by.api_boundary (they remain under realized_by.implementation; the implication keeps tracking).
- Edit .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md to remove Mix.Tasks.Spec.Check.run/1 from realized_by.api_boundary.
After these edits, run mix spec.validate (no realized_by_redundant_dup warnings should remain) and mix spec.check (no drift findings should fire from the dedup itself, because the implication continues to track).

Verification:
- Tests to pass: mix spec.validate is clean (no realized_by_redundant_dup warnings).
- Tests to pass: mix spec.check is clean (no drift findings introduced by the dedup).
- Behaviors to demonstrate: spec scenario specled.realized_by.scenario.existing_duplication_validator_warns end-to-end (warning fires before edit, gone after edit).

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/** (no production code changes)
- test/** (no test changes)
Allowed touches:
- .spec/specs/binding.spec.md
- .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT touch realized_by.spec.md (it was extended at distill time and stays).
- Do NOT touch the new ADR (it was authored at distill time).
- Do NOT add new requirements in this stage; this is purely the visible-effect-of-the-feature edit.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED

Stage: specled_-701.10/spec-doc-updates-author-dedup
Worktree: /Users/mz/src/specled_ex-701-10
Commit: c248a40 specled_-701.10: author-side dedup of redundant api_boundary entries

Edits (allowed touches only):
- .spec/specs/binding.spec.md — removed 3 MFAs (Binding.resolve/2, Canonical.hash/1, HashStore.read/2) from realized_by.api_boundary; the api_boundary tier was the only consumer so the empty key was dropped. Implication still tracks them via implementation.
- .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md — removed Mix.Tasks.Spec.Check.run/1 from realized_by.api_boundary (also dropped the now-empty api_boundary key). Implication still tracks it via implementation. Also flipped the dedup_realized_by_* tagged_tests verification stub from execute: false to execute: true (the corresponding tests already exist and pass).

Verification:
- mix spec.validate: status=pass errors=0 warnings=1. The four redundant_dup warnings on this stage's subjects (3 on specled.binding + 1 on specled.mix_tasks) are gone. The 1 residual warning is on specled.realized_by where expand_implications/1 self-demonstrates the dup at distill time — realized_by.spec.md is explicitly out of scope for this stage.
- mix spec.check: branch findings (16) are pre-existing epic-wide branch_guard / overlap drift unrelated to this stage's edits. Pre-edit baseline was 20 findings; the dedup actually cleared 4 (the redundant_dup warnings). No new drift findings (no branch_guard_realization_drift, no branch_guard_dangling_binding) were introduced — the implication continues to track the dedup'd entries.
- mix test test/specled_ex/mix/tasks/spec_dedup_realized_by_test.exs: 11 tests, 0 failures (confirms the verification-stub flip is sound).

Behavior demonstrated end-to-end: specled.realized_by.scenario.existing_duplication_validator_warns — warnings fired before edit (5 total), gone for the dedup'd entries after edit (4 cleared).

VERIFY: PASS
Session: eeca2123-30e1-430c-90c1-08dd23adf991
Log: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/eeca2123-30e1-430c-90c1-08dd23adf991.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 3 (.spec/specs/binding.spec.md, .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md, .spec/state.json)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 1 (.spec/state.json — known regeneration artifact from running mix spec.validate; ticket Allowed Touches lists only the two spec.md files but the verifier instructions explicitly call this out as expected)
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs (lib/**, test/**): 0

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 1 (the dedup_realized_by_* tagged_tests stub was flipped from execute: false to execute: true in mix_tasks.spec.md), passed: 1 (mix test test/specled_ex/mix/tasks/spec_dedup_realized_by_test.exs — 11 tests, 0 failures, per implementer)
- Requirements with passing tests: full suite 768 tests, 0 failures (mix test green)
- Intent-level scope concerns: none
- Project verification: pass

Detail:
- mix spec.validate: status=pass, errors=0, warnings=1. The 4 net-new realized_by_redundant_dup warnings introduced by S7's check on the project's own specs (specled.binding x3 — Binding.resolve/2, Canonical.hash/1, HashStore.read/2; specled.mix_tasks x1 — Mix.Tasks.Spec.Check.run/1) are CLEARED. The lone residual warning is on specled.realized_by for SpecLedEx.Realization.EffectiveBinding.expand_implications/1, where realized_by.spec.md self-demonstrates the dup at distill time.
- mix spec.check: 16 branch findings (branch_guard_missing_subject_update + branch_guard_unmapped_change + 3 overlap/duplicate_covers). All pre-existing epic-wide drift unrelated to this stage's two-spec edit. No new branch_guard_realization_drift or branch_guard_dangling_binding fired for any dedup'd MFA — implication continues to track them via implementation, exactly as the ADR specifies.
- Source-claim check (manual): .spec/specs/binding.spec.md realized_by no longer contains the api_boundary key; the 3 MFAs (Binding.resolve/2, Canonical.hash/1, HashStore.read/2) remain present under implementation. Same for .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md: api_boundary key dropped; Mix.Tasks.Spec.Check.run/1 remains under implementation. Confirmed by reading both files post-edit.
- Pre/post baseline (via git show HEAD~1:.spec/specs/...): pre-edit binding.spec.md listed all 3 MFAs under api_boundary; pre-edit mix_tasks.spec.md listed Mix.Tasks.Spec.Check.run/1 under api_boundary. Post-edit they are gone from api_boundary and the implication keeps them tracked. End-to-end demonstration of specled.realized_by.scenario.existing_duplication_validator_warns is sound.

Verdict: PASS

Notes for auditor:
- The lone residual realized_by_redundant_dup warning on specled.realized_by (self-dup of expand_implications/1 in realized_by.spec.md) is ACCEPTABLE per the ticket's "Out of Scope (intent): Do NOT touch realized_by.spec.md (it was extended at distill time and stays)." The ticket explicitly carves out realized_by.spec.md from this stage's edits, and this scenario subject deliberately keeps the dup as live in-spec demonstration of the warning behavior. Removing it would violate the stated intent boundary.
- .spec/state.json is in the diff (~298 line reduction). This is the expected regeneration artifact called out by the verifier instructions ("the implementer's commit may include .spec/state.json regeneration — that's a known artifact of running mix spec.validate"). The delta consists of cleared findings from prior epic stages whose surfaces and tagged tests now exist (S6 orchestrator wiring, S7 validator dedup check, S8 mix task, S9 supporting fixtures) — i.e., the regeneration reflects already-merged-in epic work rather than this stage's edits. Not introduced by this stage.
- Branch finding "branch_guard_unmapped_change lib/specled_ex/validator/realized_by_dedup_check.ex" and similar are pre-existing epic-wide drift; not new in this stage.
- Stub flip (execute: false -> true) on dedup_realized_by_* tagged_tests in mix_tasks.spec.md is in-bounds: tests already exist (S9 landed them) and pass.

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: agent-a960a40815bfc4d2e (subagent of orchestrator session eeca2123-30e1-430c-90c1-08dd23adf991)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/eeca2123-30e1-430c-90c1-08dd23adf991/subagents/agent-a960a40815bfc4d2e.jsonl

Reasoning: Diff against e0c9585 is exactly the two allowed-touch spec.md files plus a regenerated .spec/state.json. The two spec edits (.spec/specs/binding.spec.md L27-33, .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md L35-46) drop the api_boundary key entirely and keep all 4 MFAs (Binding.resolve/2, Canonical.hash/1, HashStore.read/2, Mix.Tasks.Spec.Check.run/1) under implementation, so the implication continues to track them — verified by `mix spec.check` showing zero `branch_guard_realization_drift` / zero `branch_guard_dangling_binding` for any dedup'd MFA (the 16 branch findings are all pre-existing epic-wide branch_guard / overlap drift). The .spec/state.json delta is purely regeneration: -19 requirement_without_test_tag + -19 tagged_tests_cover_missing_tag + -1 surface_target_missing (cleared as S6/S7/S8/S9 surfaces and tagged tests came in upstream) and +1 realized_by_redundant_dup for the realized_by.spec.md self-dup that's intentionally retained — no edit-related artifacts that should have been a separate decision. End-to-end demonstration of specled.realized_by.scenario.existing_duplication_validator_warns is sound: pre-edit (git show e0c9585b:.spec/specs/{binding,mix_tasks}.spec.md) has all 4 MFAs duplicated under api_boundary+implementation triggering 4 net-new realized_by_redundant_dup warnings; post-edit `mix spec.validate` returns status=pass, errors=0, warnings=1 with the lone residual being EffectiveBinding.expand_implications/1 on specled.realized_by (out-of-scope per the ticket's intent carve-out and kept as live in-spec demo). Stub flip (mix_tasks.spec.md L196-202 execute: false → true) is justified: `mix test test/specled_ex/mix/tasks/spec_dedup_realized_by_test.exs` runs 11/0, full suite 768/0.
APPROVED. Merged into specled_-701/realized-by-tier-implication. Closing per orchestrator sign-off.

title: S9: ADR + spec doc updates + author-side dedup
